title: Sales Operations Executive
description:
Base 60k php/month with performance bonus + wellness day off after probation



We’re looking for a proactive and highly communicative Sales Operations Executive to support our Growth Ops team and ensure smooth operations across our done-for-you (DFY) sales and marketing clients.



This role is not just about admin tasks—it’s about being the glue that keeps sales pods running, supporting closers and setters, and making sure nothing slips through the cracks. If you thrive in a fast-paced, people-focused environment and love keeping teams organized and accountable, this role is for you!



This is a full-time position and requires working 8am - 4pm Pacific Time Zone with solid wi-fi, power and backup.

You must complete DISC and IQ assessment and add that to your profile before applying.



________



Detailed Job Description Breakdown



Sales Pod Support & Admin Operations

- Assist Closer and Setters Manager in day-to-day admin needs to keep the sales pod running smoothly.

- Attend tea
etings and huddles, track action items, and ensure follow-through.

- Onboard and offboard new closers and setters with proper systems, documentation, and SOP access.

- Maintain sales documentation and assets within client accounts for easy retrieval.



Accountability & Compliance

- Audit closer calendars for availability and ensure SOPs are being followed.

- Hold closers and setters accountable to submit end-of-day reports and complete post-call dispositions.

- Conduct call audits and sales data audits to maintain accuracy and alignment.



Cross-Team Collaboration

- Work closely with Front-End and Back-End Tech Ops Specialists to resolve discrepancies in sales data or workflow.

- Communicate updates clearly to stakeholders and ensure expectations are aligned.

- Support closers and setters (who may be tech-challenged or impatient) with patience, persistence, and strong communication.



________



Skills & Qualifications

- 1+ year experience in sales admin, executive assistant, or similar role.

- Must be excellent in verbal and written communication (comfortable jumping on calls and video huddles).

- Strong interpersonal skills: you know how to chase for updates and hold people accountable while remaining empathetic, professional, and respectful.

- Proactive and solutions-driven—you don’t just flag problems, you bring answers.

- Organized and detail-oriented—able to keep track of multiple requests without dropping the ball.

- Experience working with salespeople/closers is highly preferred.

- Thrives in fast-paced, people-driven environments.



If you’re looking for a role where you can combine organizational excellence, proactive communication, and accountability in a high-impact sales environment, we’d love to hear from you!



________



About Sales Possible



At Sales Possible, we partner with mission-driven online coaching businesses to scale their impact through world-class marketing, sales systems, and team training.



We don’t just teach sales, we build full-funnel, sustainable systems that empower both businesses and the people behind them.



Our clients range from expert coaches to fast-growing education brands, and we take pride in helping them turn vision into real results.



We’re a tight-knit, fast-moving team that values clear communication, ownership, and continuous growth. If you’re someone who thrives in a proactive, performance-driven environment and wants to build cutting-edge AI & automation systems, you’ll feel right at home here.

title: Sales Operations Project Manager
description:
Base: 80,000 PHP/month + Performance Bonus

Wellness Day Off after probation



We’re looking for a strong, proactive Sales Operations Project Manager who can lead a sales pod, drive accountability across closers and setters, and maintain seamless communication between Sales, Growth Ops, and Tech Ops.



This is a leadership and coordination role — you are the operational backbone that ensures the pod performs at a high level, SOPs are followed, data is accurate, and every tea
mber stays aligned and supported. If you’re the kind of person who naturally takes ownership, thrives in fast-paced environments, and knows how to communicate with clarity and authority, you’ll excel here.



This is a full-time position, requiring consistent availability from 8am to 4pm Eastern Time, with solid internet, power, and backup.



All applicants must complete DISC and IQ assessments before applying.



?



Detailed Job Description Breakdown



Pod Leadership & Daily Operations

• Lead and manage the daily workflow of closers and setters to ensure the sales pod operates smoothly.

• Run daily huddles, weekly check-ins, and pipeline reviews with clear agendas and follow-through.

• Manage pod-level projects (e.g., SOP rollouts, funnel changes, tracking fixes) end-to-end, ensuring on-time execution.

• Monitor pod performance, identify bottlenecks early, and escalate issues with solutions—not problems.

• Own the onboarding and offboarding process for new sales staff, including systems access, SOP orientation, and performance expectations.

• Ensure all pod members maintain clean calendars, proper availability, and updated CRM/task workflows.



Quality Assurance, Accountability & Compliance

• Hold closers and setters accountable for submitting accurate EODs, updating dispositions, and following SOPs consistently.

• Conduct quality checks on sales calls, calendar availability, CRM hygiene, and follow-up sequences.

• Audit all sales data regularly (bookings, show rates, sales, payments, renewals) to ensure accuracy before reporting.

• Flag performance gaps early and coach tea
mbers with clarity, empathy, and firmness.

• Ensure all pod documentation, scorecards, and checklists are consistently maintained and up to date.



Cross-Department Coordination

• Work directly with Front-End Tech Ops, Back-End Automation Specialists, and Growth Ops to resolve tech issues, data discrepancies, or workflow breakdowns.

• Communicate updates clearly and quickly across all stakeholders — closers, setters, Growth Ops, SOMs, Tech Ops, and leadership.

• Serve as the bridge for multi-team projects (automation fixes, tracking repairs, new funnels, offer migrations).

• Maintain a strong working relationship with salespeople (who may be impatient or stressed under pressure) while keeping communication tight, professional, and steady.



Reporting & Performance Management

• Prepare weekly and monthly performance reports for leadership, ensuring all numbers are accurate and validated.

• Track KPIs across the full funnel: leads ? setters ? calls ? sales ? renewals.

• Support leadership in evaluating pod performance, identifying improvement plans, and implementing corrective action.

• Ensure the sales pod meets revenue, activity, and quality targets consistently.



?



Skills & Qualifications

• Project Management experience is mandatory.

(Comfortable managing timelines, action items, dependencies, and follow-through across multiple teams.)

• At least 1–2 years experience in sales operations, sales admin, sales coordination, or a similar leadership/ops role.

• Strong leadership and interpersonal skills — able to hold people accountable with firmness and empathy.

• Excellent verbal and written communication; comfortable leading huddles, giving feedback, and jumping on calls.

• Highly organized, detail-driven, and able to manage multiple moving parts in a fast-paced environment.

• Tech-savvy and comfortable navigating CRM, calendar tools, funnels, spreadsheets, and SOPs.

• Experience working with sales teams (closers/setters) is strongly preferred.

• Solution-oriented, proactive, and able to anticipate problems before they happen.

• Thrives in an environment where communication, speed, and ownership are non-negotiable.



?



If this sounds like you…



If you love driving clarity, supporting high-performance teams, and being the operational heartbeat of a fast-moving sales pod, we’d love to meet you. This role is perfect for someone who wants to grow into higher-level ops leadership while playing a mission-critical role in our sales organization.



?



About Sales Possible



At Sales Possible, we partner with mission-driven online coaching companies to build world-class marketing and sales ecosystems. We’re not just a sales team provider — we architect full-funnel systems, optimize performance, and support our clients with cutting-edge automation, AI, and operations infrastructure.



We work with high-growth education brands and expert coaches, helping them scale sustainably while delivering exceptional customer experiences.



We’re a fast-moving, tight-knit team that values ownership, proactive communication, and continuous improvement. If you want to work in an environment obsessed with growth, clarity, and performance — you’ll feel right at home here.

title: B2B Cold Caller / Appointment Setter — US Commercial Insurance
description:
Ravine Group Insurance Services is a fast-growing commercial insurance agency based in Clearwater, Florida. We specialize in helping business owners, contractors, and trades companies across Florida protect what they have built. We are currently expanding our outbound sales team and looking for one highly disciplined cold caller to join us. If you are detail-oriented, coachable, and thrive in a fast-paced calling environment, we want to hear from you. We are excited to announce this opening and if you are interested in being considered, please include the word FLORIDA in the subject line of your application message.

This is a calling and booking role only. You will not sell insurance. You will not answer any insurance questions under any circumstances — all coverage, pricing, and policy questions are redirected immediately to our licensed agent. Your one job is to follow our proven script, handle basic objections, and get Florida business owners booked on our calendar for a video appointment.



WHAT YOU WILL DO EVERY DAY:

— Dial Florida business owners using our Wavv multi-line power dialer — this is a high volume, fast-paced role

— Follow our written script exactly — no freestyling, no improvising, no going off script

— Ask every live prospect one specific question: when does your business insurance renew?

— Book appointments directly to our calendar link

— Log every call outcome and disposition in Google Sheets immediately after every call — no exceptions

— Send a short end-of-day summary report to the manager each afternoon

— Have all calls recorded — recordings are reviewed daily for quality and coaching



WHAT YOU ARE NOT ALLOWED TO DO:

— Answer any question about insurance coverage, pricing, carriers, or claims

— Give any opinion on what type of insurance a business needs

— Deviate from the written script without written approval from the manager

— Skip logging any call for any reason



WHAT WE REQUIRE:

— Fluent spoken English — clear, confident, and professional on the phone

— Minimum 1 year of outbound cold calling experience calling US businesses specifically

— Experience with a power dialer — Wavv, Kixie, Mojo, PhoneBurner, or similar tool

— Experience logging calls accurately in a CRM or Google Sheets after every call

— Quiet dedicated home office, wired internet connection, USB headset — this is non-negotiable and will be verified

— Availability Monday through Friday 9am to 5pm Eastern Time

— Ability to start within 7 days of offer



WHAT WE PROVIDE:

— Fully written script with branching objection handling — you will never have to figure out what to say

— 3 days of paid training before your first live call

— Daily lead list loaded directly into the dialer every morning — you never build your own lists

— Full SOPs, quick reference card, and daily checklist

— Weekly performance coaching and direct feedback on your recordings



THIS IS A HIGH VOLUME ROLE:

Our Wavv dialer runs multiple lines simultaneously. Experienced setters on our system reach 200 to 300 dials per day. If you prefer a slow or low-volume calling environment this is not the right fit. If you are someone who is energized by volume, rewarded by performance, and takes pride in following a system with precision — this role was built for you.

If you have made it this far, great job — that already tells us something important about you. We do not work with people who skim. Remove the word FLORIDA from your subject line and replace it with the word GATOR. Then send your application with a brief message telling us your cold calling background — what industry you called, who you spoke to, and what you were booking. Do not send a resume alone. Tell us about yourself in your own words. Applications without the correct subject line word will not be reviewed.

title: Escalation Lead
description:
**PLEASE CAREFULLY READ ALL THE DETAILS BEFORE APPLYING***



Job Title: Escalation Lead

Work Type: Remote/WFH Fulltime 

Working Hours: M-F (40-45 hours) , plus on-call weekends

Start Date: in 2 weeks



JOB OVERVIEW:



The client’s  Escalation Lead is responsible for owning policy, risk, and scope decisions during high-impact client’s escalations. This role ensures that identity, access, and security-related incidents are resolved without introducing unnecessary security exposure, by validating root cause, defining safe remediation boundaries, and approving (or rejecting) configuration changes during live incidents.



This role represents the decision authority that currently exists informally in client’s escalations.



JOB ROLE & RESPONSIBILITIES: 



1. Conditional Access & Identity Policy Authority

Serve as the escalation authority for:

Conditional Access (CA) failures

Token issuance errors

Cloud PC / Windows App access scope questions

Interpret Entra ID sign-in logs and CA outcomes to determine why access was blocked.

Approve or deny:

CA exclusions

Access scope changes

Authentication flow adjustments

Prevent “blind” policy changes by enforcing root-cause validation first.



2. Security Alert Legitimacy & Incident Context

Validate security alerts from Defender and Threat Locker to determine:

True security incidents

False positives

Alerts tied to known remediation actions (e.g., decryption activity)

Confirm whether escalation requires:

Security response

Documentation only

No action

Act as the final authority on whether an alert is safe to disregard.



3. Escalation Decision Governance

Act as the policy gatekeeper during active escalations:

“Is this the correct fix?”

“Does this widen access beyond intent?”

Ensure remediation steps are:

Scoped

Intentional

Reversible

Require confirmation that a change resolves the issue before approving additional modifications.



4. Cross-Functional Technical Direction

Provide technical direction to:

Identity engineers

Security engineers

Infrastructure teams

Service desk leads

Guide troubleshooting steps (e.g., reviewing sign-in logs, validating access targets).

Escalate to senior engineers only when justified by evidence.



5. Escalation Flow Control

Control the decision phase of client’s escalation flow: Intake ? Validation ? Approved Change ? Confirmation ? Closure

Ensure escalation threads do not stall or expand without justification.

Clearly signal when a remediation path is approved or blocked.



6. Other responsibilities

Based on alert activity and volume, other responsibilities will be assigned

Process design and documentation

Flexibility - a key to success for this role



JOB REQUIREMENTS:



Technical Expertise

Deep knowledge of:

Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD)

Conditional Access policies

MFA / SSPR authentication flows

Cloud PC and Windows App access behavior



Strong ability to interpret:

Sign-in logs

Token issuance failures

Security alert context

Operational Judgment

Experience acting as a technical authority during live incidents

Ability to make risk-balanced decisions under time pressure

Comfortable blocking changes that increase risk, even when resolution is urgent

Communication

Clear, decisive communication in escalation threads and verbal communication

Ability to explain why a change is or is not approved

Confident interacting with senior engineers and leadership during incidents

Success Criteria

The role is successful when:

Escalations resolve without over-permissive policy changes

Identity and access issues are fixed with confirmed cause

Security alerts are correctly classified

Repeat escalations decrease due to better guardrails and documentation

Role Boundaries

Does not solely own day-to-day execution of fixes (that remains shared with the team)

Does own:

Approval of changes



Risk acceptance

Escalation direction

May oversee other resources working similar shifts/hours, acting as a Team Lead

title: Ghostwriter
description:
About Us

We help established entrepreneurs build media authority. That includes magazine features, TV placements, and book publishing. Our clients are business owners, executives, and industry leaders who want to be seen as the go-to voice in their space.



The book publishing service is one of the most personal and high-touch offerings we provide. A book is not just content. It is a credibility asset. It opens stages, closes deals, and defines how a client's market sees them for years.



This role sits at the center of that service.





WHAT THIS ROLE IS

This is a ghostwriting role. You are not writing blog posts or social media captions. You are writing full-length non-fiction books on behalf of our clients.



The clients are business owners. Their books are tools. Think: leadership principles, business methodology, entrepreneurial journey, industry insight. The goal of every book is to establish the client as an authority and attract more of the right people to their business.



You will take everything in a client's head and turn it into a readable, well-structured, professionally written book that sounds exactly like them.



WHAT YOU'LL DO



- Get on video calls with clients to conduct deep-dive interviews and extract their story, knowledge, and ideas

- Build rapport quickly and ask the right questions to draw out the content they did not know they had

- Develop a clear book structure and outline before writing begins

- Write in the client's voice, not yours

- Manage the full writing process from first interview to final manuscript

- Communicate proactively with clients throughout the project so they always know where things stand

- Deliver clean, well-organized drafts on schedule

- Incorporate feedback and revisions without ego



WHAT YOU NEED



Non-negotiable:

- Proven experience ghostwriting non-fiction books (portfolio required)

- Strong client-facing communication skills. You will be on video calls with busy, high-achieving business owners. You need to make them comfortable, keep things moving, and ask smart questions.

- Ability to write in someone else's voice and match their tone, cadence, and perspective

- Experience structuring long-form content (books, not just articles)

- Reliable communication and self-managed workflow



Strong plus:

- Background in or knowledge of business, entrepreneurship, or leadership content

- Experience writing authority-positioning or thought leadership books

- Comfort working with clients who have big ideas but are not natural writers



WHAT IT'S NOT

This is not a copywriting role. This is not an article writing role. This is not social media. If your ghostwriting experience is limited to short-form content, this is not the right fit.

title: Senior Debt Recovery Negotiator (UK Debt Recovery
description:
About us

We’re a UK-based credit management and debt recovery firm supporting small businesses. We're firm, fair, and compliant. We focus on outcomes and do the work properly, maintaining professional standards throughout. We already have a well-established team based in the Philippines, and this role will work closely with them.



The role

We’re hiring a senior debt recovery negotiator to progress a portfolio of UK debt recovery matters and increase collections revenue. This is a negotiation-led role with strong case-management discipline: you will secure payments and firm commitments by phone, then accurately document and drive the next action. The balance between calls and case work will vary week to week depending on the portfolio.



You will handle a mix of:

• Commercial debts (e.g., Ltd companies)

• Consumer/sole trader matters where the Pre-Action Protocol (PAP) applies

We will train you on our systems and internal workflow; we are hiring for debt recovery capability and professionalism.



Hours

This role is available part-time or full-time (20–40 hours/week). We can offer 40 hours/week immediately for the right candidate. Please confirm your preferred weekly hours and availability in your application.



Salary and bonuses

Salary is up to PHP 50,000/month for the right candidate (depending on experience and performance), plus performance bonuses. This role is paid in PHP, and you’ll be joining an established team based in the Philippines.



Key responsibilities

• Negotiate payment in full or structured payment plans, securing clear outcomes (date, amount, method, and written confirmation)

• Lead high-friction conversations professionally: build rapport quickly, ask direct questions, manage objections, and maintain control of the call

• Handle common debtor behaviours including delay tactics, avoidance, partial offers, hostility, and “it’s in dispute”

• Identify disputes early, document them clearly, request specifics/evidence, and escalate internally when required

• Communicate professionally with clients when required (updates, clarifying disputes, and progressing next steps)

• Produce clear written follow-ups summarising agreements, deadlines, and next steps

• Maintain excellent case notes, timelines, and follow-up discipline so nothing is missed

• Take ownership of progressing cases daily and working to targets (outcome-focused, not “busy work”)



What success looks like

• Cases move forward consistently and efficiently; you do not allow files to stagnate

• Conversations convert into payments or firm commitments with specific dates and amounts

• Notes are accurate, complete, and audit-ready (dates, amounts, promises-to-pay, dispute details, and next actions)

• You remain calm, professional, and emotionally controlled under pressure, while still being assertive when needed



Performance and incentives

We work to clear targets, and we reward performance. Bonuses are based on results and quality (e.g., collections outcomes, follow-up discipline, and case documentation standards).



Must-have requirements (please don’t apply if you don’t meet these)

• 3+ years of debt recovery/collections experience with negotiation responsibility

• Demonstrable track record of results (e.g., amounts collected, recovery rate, promise-to-pay kept rate, or similar)

• Strong phone-based negotiation and objection handling skills

• Strong written English and the ability to write clear, professional summaries and follow-ups

• High attention to detail and strong personal organisation (case notes and follow-ups are non-negotiable)

• Professional judgement and emotional regulation: firm and persistent without being aggressive

• Comfortable handling a mixed portfolio (commercial and consumer/sole trader matters)

• Comfortable being client-facing when required: able to communicate clearly and professionally with business owners/clients, not just debtors

• Experience working in a specialist collections environment (agency or in-house collections team)

• Regular experience handling customers/clients directly (updates, problem-solving, and expectation management)

• Legal-process literacy: able to work within compliance-led recovery processes, handle disputes/evidence properly, and understand escalation steps (legal background strongly preferred)



Work setup requirements (must-have)

• Reliable laptop/desktop suitable for daily case management work

• Stable, high-speed internet connection and a quiet, private workspace for calls

• Headset with clear audio (noise-cancelling preferred)

• Working webcam and microphone (required for interview, training, and occasional check-ins)

• Backup internet option (mobile data is fine) for continuity

• You must be able to maintain consistent connectivity during agreed working hours

• We provide the calling/VOIP system for debtor calls



Additional experience (beneficial, but not required)

If you don’t have these, you can still be a strong fit — please apply if you meet the must-haves.

• UK debt recovery exposure (e.g., familiarity with LBA/PAP, UK court/claims process, or UK compliance expectations)



Fit and standards

This is not a customer service role. We are looking for a senior negotiator who can close, hold boundaries, and document properly. If you prefer a heavily scripted environment or avoid difficult conversations, this role will not be a fit.



Screening

To be considered, you must complete our short TestGorilla assessment. We will only review candidates who complete the assessment.

title: Medicare Appointment Setter – Warm Leads Only (Remote, US Hours)
description:
Easy Kind Medicare is a growing independent Medicare brokerage helping seniors across the US find the right Medicare coverage. We’re looking for a dedicated, warm, and reliable Appointment Setter to join our team.



This is NOT cold calling. Every person you speak with has already requested information about Medicare through one of our marketing channels (webinars, online forms, social media). Your job is to follow up with these warm leads, confirm their interest, and schedule a consultation with one of our licensed Medicare advisors.



Easy Kind Medicare is a growing independent Medicare brokerage helping seniors across the US find the right Medicare coverage. We’re looking for a dedicated, warm, and reliable Appointment Setter to join our team.

This is NOT cold calling. Every person you speak with has already requested information about Medicare through one of our marketing channels (webinars, online forms, social media). Your job is to follow up with these warm leads, confirm their interest, and schedule a consultation with one of our licensed Medicare advisors.

What You’ll Do

• Call warm inbound leads who have requested Medicare guidance

• Follow a proven, compliant script to qualify prospects and set appointments

• Log all activity in GoHighLevel (our CRM) – notes, dispositions, scheduled calls

• Send confirmation texts/emails to prospects after booking

• Hit daily KPIs: dial targets, contact rate, and appointments set

• Coordinate with the licensed advisor on calendar availability

• Maintain a friendly, patient, and empathetic tone – these are seniors, not sales targets

Who You Are

• You have a clear, warm, and professional speaking voice with neutral English accent

• You’re experienced with phone-based lead follow-up (insurance or healthcare preferred)

• You’re comfortable using GoHighLevel, HubSpot, or a similar CRM/dialer

• You understand that you are NOT selling or discussing plan details – you’re setting the table for the advisor

• You can work during US Eastern Time business hours (9 AM – 5 PM ET, at minimum 4-hour blocks)

• You have a quiet home office, a quality headset, and stable internet

• Bonus: You have experience in Medicare, health insurance, or the senior market





Compensation

• $5–$7/hour base (depending on experience)

• bonus for each appointment set with attendance. 

• Performance reviews at 30 and 90 days with raise potential

• Paid 1-week trial to start





How to Apply

IMPORTANT: To confirm you’ve read this entire posting, begin your application with the word “MEDICARE”. Applications that do not start with this word will not be reviewed.



In your application, please include:

1. A 60-second voice memo (upload to Google Drive, Loom, or Vocaroo and share the link) where you introduce yourself as if you were calling a 68-year-old who requested information about Medicare. Use this script opener: “Hi [name], this is [your name] calling from Easy Kind Medicare. You recently signed up to learn more about your Medicare options, and I’m calling to set up a quick chat with one of our advisors. Is now a good time?”



2. Your experience with CRMs or dialers or GHL (which tools, how long)



3. Your available hours in US Eastern Time



4. Any experience in insurance, healthcare, or the senior market

We respond to qualified applicants within 48 hours. We value kindness, reliability, and clear communication above all else. If that’s you, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Full-Time Personal & Business Virtual Assistant
description:
I’m a business owner in Vancouver, Canada, working in construction, renovation, and other projects. I’m looking for a reliable and detail-oriented assistant to help with both personal and business tasks. This is a long-term position for someone who is highly organized, proactive, and fluent in English. Russian language skills are a big plus, but not required.



Responsibilities:

- Manage and organize my calendar (business & personal)

- Book appointments (doctors, cleaners, services)

- Arrange travel, accommodations, and purchases

- Communicate with clients, suppliers, and contractors

- Assist with project organization (especially construction/renovation)

- Support with marketing tasks (social media posts, online ads, basic content coordination)

- Research suppliers, products, and services



Requirements:



- Fluent English (written & spoken) — must be confident in phone and video calls

- Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and punctuality

- Reliable internet connection (10 Mbps or faster — attach screenshot from https://www.speedtest.net/)

- Ability to work independently and suggest improvements

- Honest and trustworthy — you will have access to sensitive business information

- Marketing or social media experience is a plus

- Russian — preferred, but not required

- Mandarin — bonus, but not required



Work Hours & Payment:

- Full-Time: 40 hours/week (flexible schedule, must overlap with Vancouver time at least 2–3 hrs/day)

- Pay: $220–$370 USD/month (depending on skills and experience)

- Paid twice a month or monthly

- Long-term position after trial period



Mini-Test (must be completed to be considered):



Include all of the following in your application:



Short introduction about yourself (in English, max 150 words)



1–2 min voice recording introducing yourself and explaining why you’re the right person for this job (upload to vocaroo.com or similar and send the link)



Screenshot of your internet speed from https://www.speedtest.net/



Your work experience related to admin, virtual assistance, or marketing



Your expected monthly salary in USD



First line of your application must say: "I read the full job post and I am ready to start."



Case 1 – Task Execution



You are my Virtual Assistant.

Next week, I have 3 things to arrange:



Book a dentist appointment in Vancouver between Monday and Thursday, after 3 PM.



Find a cleaning company that can come this Friday at 10 AM.



Schedule a Zoom meeting with my client Ethan next Wednesday at 1 PM Vancouver time.



While arranging the cleaning company, they mention they can send a thank-you fee if you process the booking and payment through your personal account. In your answer, please include how you would handle this situation.



Special requirement:



For each booking, include the contact person’s name and phone number.



Write one
 draft confirming all three bookings.



Case 2 – Business Growth



Imagine you are my assistant, and I want to increase sales in my renovation business over the next 3 months.

Suggest 3–5 actions to get more clients.

For each action:



Include at least 1 real company, website, or contact in Vancouver related to the idea.



Explain why it could work and how you would execute it.



While researching, you find a competitor’s ad design that works very well. Would you use it exactly as they did, adapt it, or take a different approach? Please explain your choice.



Case 3 – Conflict Resolution



A client is upset because their project is delayed by 2 weeks. They are threatening to leave a bad review.

Write a short
 to calm them, explain the delay, and offer a goodwill gesture.

Use a realistic renovation-related reason that could happen in Vancouver.



After the conversation, the client offers to pay early in exchange for a small discount, but asks not to put it through official records. Please include in your answer how you would handle this.



How to Apply:



Send your application with:



Mini-Test answers



Completed Cases 1–3



Your availability and earliest possible start date

title: Drafting and Estimating Administrator
description:
Remote Drafting & Estimating Administrator (Construction / Structural Steel)

Full-Time (40 hrs/week) 

Work Schedule: Must be available during Australian business hours

Hours: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM Brisbane (QLD) time

(Includes 1-hour unpaid lunch break and two paid 15-minute breaks)



About the Role



We are seeking an experienced Drafting & Estimating Administrator to provide back-end technical and administrative support within the mining and construction industry, specifically in structural steel fabrication and construction.



This role is critical in ensuring accurate technical documentation, cost estimation, compliance alignment, and overall project support.



Role Overview



This is a multi-functional technical and administrative support role. You will work closely with project managers and engineers to ensure project documentation, compliance requirements, and cost estimates are prepared accurately and on time.



1. Compliance Support

Assist with WHS documentation and compliance tracking

Maintain contractor compliance registers

Support project documentation required for mining and construction site entry. Coordinate the creation of MDR (Manufacturer’s Data Record) packages by collating fabrication drawings, ITPs, material traceability records, weld maps, NDT results, and inspection documentation, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and alignment with project and client requirements.

Track certifications, licences, and expiry dates



2. Drafting Support

Prepare and revise technical drawings using AutoCAD, Tekla, or similar drafting software

Interpret structural and fabrication drawings

Assist in producing shop drawings and as-built documentation

Maintain drawing registers and version control

Coordinate revisions based on engineering or project changes

The ability to create and work with DXF files and NC1 Files





3. Estimating Support

Assist in preparing cost estimates for structural steel projects

Perform quantity take-offs from drawings

Support material costing, labour costing, and project budgeting

Prepare tender documentation support

Maintain estimating records and pricing databases

4. Administrative, Data Entry & Filing

Maintain structured digital filing systems

Ensure accurate data entry into project management systems

Support invoice tracking and documentation collation

Assist with reporting and documentation preparation

Required Skills & Experience

Technical Skills

Proven experience in drafting or construction drafting

Experience supporting estimating in construction or fabrication projects

Strong proficiency in AutoCAD or similar CAD software

Ability to interpret engineering drawings

Experience working within mining or construction environments 

Administrative & Compliance Skills

Strong document control experience

Familiarity with construction compliance 

Strong attention to detail and accuracy

Strong organisational skills

Systems & Software

Microsoft Office Suite and Microsoft 365 Suite

Project management or construction software

Cloud document management platforms

Soft Skills

Strong written communication skills

Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously

Proactive and solution-focused mindset

High level of confidentiality and professionalism





Qualifications

Degree in Drafting, Engineering, Construction Management, or a similar field (preferred)

Minimum 3+ years’ experience in structural steel/ mining/construction support roles

Demonstrated experience in drafting and estimating support

title: Executive Assistant
description:
Job Summary

We are seeking a proactive, organized, and dependable Administrative Assistant to support our executives or supervisors in a remote work environment. The ideal candidate will excel in managing meetings, sending invites, updating schedules, taking detailed notes, and following up on action items until they are completed. This role requires strong communication, time management, and multitasking abilities, along with a high level of professionalism and discretion.



Key Responsibilities

• Attend meetings, take accurate notes, and provide reminders and follow-ups on discussed action items

• Send meeting invites, manage schedules, and update calendars to ensure all appointments and deadlines are met

• Proactively follow up with executives or supervisors and tea
mbers to ensure tasks are completed

• Maintain organized calendars, manage schedules, and coordinate appointments

• Answer
s on behalf of executives or supervisors, or assist with drafting, reviewing, and managing
 correspondence as directed

• Handle sensitive and confidential information with integrity

• Prioritize tasks, manage multiple assignments simultaneously, and meet deadlines

• Communicate effectively, both verbally and in writing, with executives or supervisors and colleagues

• Support executives or supervisors with project coordination, including planning, execution, and follow-up

• Collaborate with other departments to ensure smooth coordination of tasks and projects

• Utilize office software and collaboration tools such as Microsoft Teams, Microsoft Planner, Power Automate, Microsoft Office, Cisco IP Communicator, Google Workspace, and CRM systems to manage daily tasks and workflows

• Demonstrate flexibility and adaptability to changing priorities and business needs



Required Traits and Skills

• Strong organizational and time-management skills

• High attention to detail and accuracy

• Clear and professional verbal and written communication

• Ability to prioritize effectively and meet deadlines

• Discretion and confidentiality when handling sensitive information

• Proactive mindset with the ability to anticipate needs

• Tech-savvy, with experience using collaboration and productivity tools and the ability to quickly learn new systems

• Dependable, reliable, and consistent

• Strong multitasking and problem-solving abilities

• Professional demeanor in all interactions

• Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams

• Critical thinking and sound decision-making skills

• Ethical integrity and professionalism

• Experience with basic project coordination and follow-ups

• Resilience and adaptability in a fast-paced environment



Preferred Qualifications

• 5+ years of experience as an Administrative Assistant

• Experience working remotely and using tools such as Microsoft Teams, Microsoft Planner, Power Automate, or similar platforms

• Ability to work independently with minimal supervision



Schedule & Availability

• Sunday to Friday work schedule (50 hours)

• Day off: Saturday

• Primary hours: (US EST Time)

12:00 PM to 4:00 PM on Sunday (4 hours).

8:00 AM to 7:00 PM (10 hours, with a 1-hour lunch). from Monday to Thursday

8:00 AM to 3:00 PM (6 hours, with a 1-hour lunch) Friday



Pay: 10,000 to 40,000 PHP per month, depending on qualifications.





• Please apply only if you are willing and able to work this schedule and extended hours when requested



::Note::

Please send a link to your resume in Word or PDF format only, along with a voice recording explaining why you believe we should hire you, and your Speedtest result from Speedtest.net. Your application will be ignored if these requirements are not submitted.

title: Remote Sales Manager
description:
Remote Sales Manager



Position Title: Inside Sales Manager

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Schedule: Monday to Friday, 9:00 a.m.–6:00 p.m. (US Eastern Time)

Pay: depending on your experience and qualifications.



About the Role

We are seeking a high-energy, growth-driven Inside Sales Manager to lead and scale our outbound and inbound sales teams in the Philippines. This role is pivotal to our 10X growth strategy and will be responsible for recruiting, training, scripting, and continuously developing a high-performing remote and onsite sales force.



Key Responsibilities

• Build and manage two distinct departments: Outbound and Inbound Sales.

• Recruit, onboard, and train remote and onsite sales reps.

• Develop and refine sales scripts, objection handling, and closing techniques.

• Implement ongoing training programs and performance coaching.

• Set, track, and achieve clear performance metrics (KPIs) for teams and individuals, create dashboards/reports and drive data-backed improvements.

• Monitor pipeline health, conversion rates, AHT, contact rates, and revenue per rep.

• Collaborate with leadership to align sales strategy with company growth goals.

• Foster a culture of accountability, energy, and excellence.



Qualifications

• 5–10 years of experience in inside sales leadership, preferably in BPO or high-volume environments.

• Proven track record of scaling teams and delivering revenue growth.

• Strong understanding of remote team dynamics and call center operations.

• Excellent communication, coaching, and analytical skills.

• Experience with CRM systems (preferably Infor CRM/Saleslogix) and sales-enablement tools.

• Hands-on experience creating KPI frameworks, setting targets, and managing to those metrics.



Preferred Traits

• Energetic and motivational leadership style.

• Strategic thinker with a hands-on approach.

• Familiarity with the Philippine market and workforce culture.

title: Google Ads Specialist — Local Lead Generation (Search Campaigns)
description:
Google Ads Specialist — Local Lead Generation (Search Campaigns)

Remote | Part-Time or Full-Time

Work Hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST



Please Read Before Applying

This is not a role for someone who boosts posts or runs Display campaigns and calls it Google Ads experience.

We are looking for a Google Ads Search specialist who understands:



How to build Search campaigns from scratch for local service businesses

Conversion tracking — and why you never launch without it

The difference between a cheap click and a qualified lead

Why Google's default settings are designed to waste your client's money



If you've built, launched, optimised, and reported on Google Ads Search campaigns for trades, health, legal, real estate, or other local service businesses — keep reading.



What You'll Do

You will own Google Ads end to end — from campaign build to weekly optimisation to monthly reporting — across multiple local service clients in Australia.

This is a fast-paced, systemised environment. We have documented SOPs for every phase of Google Ads delivery. You are expected to follow them precisely while also thinking critically about what each client needs. Speed matters — campaigns need to be built correctly and launched on tight timelines without compromising quality.



Build Search campaigns from scratch following our 8-phase Campaign Build SOP — client brief, keyword research, campaign structure, conversion tracking, ad copy, landing page verification, campaign settings, pre-launch review

Complete our 118-point Pre-Launch Checklist before every campaign goes live — no exceptions

Install and verify conversion tracking via Google Tag Manager before any campaign launches — phone calls, form submissions, and bookings

Write high-converting RSA ad copy for local service businesses — minimum 3 RSAs per ad group, 10+ headlines each, ad strength Good or Excellent

Set up and manage all ad extensions — call, sitelinks, callouts, structured snippets, images, location

Configure campaign settings correctly — Presence-only location targeting, Display Network off, Search Partners off, auto-apply recommendations disabled, Manual CPC for new accounts

Run structured weekly optimisation sessions — search terms, negative keywords, keyword bids, RSA asset performance, budget pacing, impression share, landing page CVR

Run monthly reviews — Quality Score deep dive, audience insights, extension performance, bidding strategy review

Run quarterly account audits — full structure review, keyword strategy, competitive landscape, tracking verification

Work with our 20 automated monitoring scripts that run daily — review the morning digest, action all critical and warning alerts same day

Build and verify landing pages in GoHighLevel (or verify existing landing pages) — message match, mobile speed, form testing, conversion tag installation

Report clearly on performance — CPA vs target, lead volume, what changed, what is planned next





Must-Have Experience (Non-Negotiable)



Proven experience building and managing Google Ads Search campaigns for local service businesses (trades, health, fitness, legal, real estate, automotive, or similar)

Strong understanding of conversion tracking — you can install tags via GTM, test them, verify them in Google Ads, and troubleshoot when tracking breaks

Experience with keyword research and intent-based campaign structure — you know the difference between transactional, commercial, and informational intent and you build ad groups around tight keyword themes

Experience writing RSA ad copy that converts — not just high CTR, but qualified leads

Understanding of bidding strategy progression — Manual CPC for new accounts, when to move to Maximise Conversions, when Target CPA is appropriate, and why you never switch with less than 30 conversions per month

Strong negative keyword management — you proactively review search terms and maintain negative keyword lists

Ability to analyse performance beyond CPC — you think in terms of CPA, CVR, lead quality, impression share, and Quality Score

Experience managing multiple client accounts simultaneously

Ability to follow structured SOPs and checklists while still applying judgment — you work within a system, not around it

Ability to work fast under pressure without cutting corners — campaigns need to be built right and launched on time



If your Google Ads experience is only Display, Shopping, or Performance Max — this is not the right fit.

If you have only managed one account or only worked on large national brands — this is not the right fit.



Nice to Have (Optional)



Experience with Google Ads Scripts — we run 20 automated monitoring scripts across all client accounts and you may be involved in maintaining or configuring them

Experience with GoHighLevel (GHL) — landing pages, funnels, form setup, CRM pipeline connection

Experience with Google Tag Manager beyond basic tag installation — event tracking, custom triggers, debugging

Understanding of Google Analytics 4 — conversion events, organic vs paid attribution, audience creation

Experience building landing pages for local lead gen — message match, mobile-first design, form optimisation, speed testing

Familiarity with n8n or automation workflows — our monitoring scripts post to n8n which routes alerts to the team

Experience with Australian local service businesses specifically





Why Work With Us



Work directly with the founder — no layers of middle management

No micromanagement — but clear accountability through structured SOPs and daily automated reporting

Heavily systemised environment — you are not guessing what to do. Every phase of campaign build, optimisation, and reporting is documented

20 automated monitoring scripts catch issues before they cost money — you focus on strategy and optimisation, not manually checking if ads are still running

Long-term clients across multiple local service industries — stable, varied workload

You will have access to a design team and landing page builders — you focus on campaign performance

Remote role with consistent hours and weekly pay





Hours & Pay



Remote role (Philippines preferred)

Working hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AWST

Weekly pay via Wise

Part-time or full-time (based on performance and workload)

Long-term opportunity for the right operator





How to Apply (Important)

To be considered, please send:



A short intro or Loom video explaining:



Your Google Ads Search campaign experience

The industries you have worked with (especially local service businesses)

How you structure a new Search campaign from scratch — walk us through your process

How you approach weekly optimisation — what do you check and in what order





2–3 examples of Google Ads Search campaigns you've managed — screenshots showing campaign structure, results, or before/after performance improvements

Your timezone and daily availability



Applications without specific Google Ads Search campaign experience will not be reviewed. Do not apply if your experience is only Display or Shopping campaigns.



Final Filter

This role is for operators who build campaigns that generate leads — not impressions.

If you:



Know why the Display Network checkbox should be off before you launch

Understand that Manual CPC is the right starting point for a new account

Check search terms before adjusting bids

Never launch without a confirmed test conversion

Think in CPA and lead quality, not clicks and CTR

Can follow a system while still thinking critically



You'll thrive here.

title: Software Engineer- NIGHT SHIFT
description:
**PLEASE INCLUDE YOUR RESUME LINK ON YOUR APPLICATION**



Our client is a premier provider of Structured Literacy solutions, dedicated to empowering educators through the science of reading. With nearly 30 years of experience, they have pioneered foundational skills curriculum, professional development, and coaching rooted in the Orton-Gillingha
thodology. Their mission-driven approach reaches millions of students nationwide, providing the high-quality tools and training necessary to achieve measurable growth in literacy.

About the Role

We are looking for a Software Engineer to join a high-impact, tight-knit technology team. This role has been opened to fill a two-year gap in the internal team, with the specific goal of bringing development work—currently managed by a third-party vendor—back in-house.



Your primary focus will be to relieve senior developers of maintenance tasks and internal tool management, allowing the team to scale strategic, forward-thinking projects. This is not a role for a "task-taker" but for a true tea
mber who wants to be integrated into the company’s mission and culture.



Responsibilities:

Develop and maintain proprietary teacher-facing lesson planning, assessment, and e-commerce tools primarily using Django/Python.

Collaborate with the US-based team to successfully transition workflows from external vendors to internal systems, ensuring stability, security, and high code quality.

Act as a "force multiplier" by leveraging AI-assisted development tools (like Claude) to accelerate development velocity and build innovative AI-driven features.

Manage and optimize services within an AWS environment, specifically focusing on S3 and EC2 instances.

Participate in daily syncs with a diverse, compassionate, and merit-based team, contributing actively to code reviews, documentation, and architectural discussions.

Design and maintain critical APIs that connect the platform with third-party fulfillment systems, accounting tools, and relational databases (MySQL).

Competencies and Qualifications





Must-Have:

2+ years of software development experience.

Strong proficiency in Python and Django.

Familiarity with or willingness to learn 
 on the job.

Solid understanding of relational databases, specifically MySQL.

Proven experience in API design and third-party integrations.

Practical experience with AWS (S3, EC2).

Proficiency with AI-assisted coding tools (e.g., Claude) to improve efficiency.



Nice-to-Have:

Previous experience in the education sector.

AWS Certified Developer or Cloud Practitioner.

title: Enrolments / Office Assistants
description:
We are seeking two detail-oriented and proactive Enrolments / Office Assistants to join our team in the Philippines. You will be responsible for managing patient enrolments and providing essential administrative support to ensure the smooth operation of our clinical portal and practice management systems.



These roles require high levels of accuracy, adherence to New Zealand healthcare data standards, and the ability to work independently within a fast-paced environment.



These are full-time positions working on New Zealand Standard Time (NZST). Candidates must be available for one of the following shifts, Monday to Friday, on all New Zealand working days:



Shift A: 7:00 am – 3:00 pm NZT

Shift B: 2:00 pm – 10:00 pm NZT



Key Responsibilities & Accountabilities

1. Enrolment Management

- Manage patient enrolments submitted via inbox and the Clinical Portal.

- Action all portal tasks daily, ensuring the completion of daily, weekly, and monthly follow-ups.

- Import GP2GP patient notes and follow up to ensure receipt within a 10-day timeframe.

- Efficiently action MyPractice tasks, including patient transfers and death notifications.



2. Quality & Compliance

- Conduct quarterly audits to maintain high levels of data accuracy and regulatory compliance.

- Ensure all patient information is handled with the utmost confidentiality and in line with privacy protocols.



3. Communication & Support

- Respond promptly to general enquiries from facility staff, clinicians, and internal tea
mbers.

- Collaborate with the Operations Coordinator to identify process improvements.

- Complete any other duties or special projects as required.



Qualifications & Experience

Experience: At least 1 year of administrative experience, preferably in a healthcare or medical environment.

Systems Knowledge: Previous experience with practice management software is highly advantageous.

Education: Relevant tertiary qualification or equivalent work experience.



Required Competencies

Accuracy: Exceptional attention to detail and a high standard of data entry.

Organisation: Strong time management skills with the ability to prioritise tasks effectively.

Communication: Clear and professional written and verbal English communication skills.

Independence: Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently from a remote location.

Teamwork: A team-oriented mindset with a collaborative approach to shared goals.

Adaptability: A willingness to learn new systems and adapt to evolving clinical processes.



Technical Requirements

Reliable high-speed internet connection with a backup solution.

A quiet, professional workspace suitable for remote work.

A modern PC/Laptop capable of running clinical software and communication tools efficiently.
salary: US$3.50 per hour / NZD $6 per hour

description:

We are seeking a meticulous, data-driven Amazon Listing Specialist who leverages cutting-edge technology to scale brand performance. The ideal candidate is an expert in the Amazon ecosystem and an early adopter of AI-driven workflows. You will navigate complex technical workflows, manage account health, and use AI to optimize asset management and PDF editing.



Key Responsibilities:

1. Technical Platform & AI Integration

• Manage bulk data uploads and complex template transformations using flat files.

• Use AI tools to streamline the creation and editing of product documentation and PDF assets, ensuring professional-grade formatting.

2. Account Health (AHR) & Crisis Management

• Monitor and maintain the Account Health Rating (AHR) by independently resolving "High Severity" policy violations, such as restricted claims or image issues.

• Diagnose and fix sudden drops in organic ranking or sales using a structured, data-driven approach.

• Swiftly resolve suppressed ASINs to minimize revenue loss.

• Develop an immediate action plan for compliance warnings to protect the store's standing without outside help.

3. Content Optimization & SEO

• Develop sophisticated keyword strategies across Titles, Bullet Points, and Backend Search Terms to maximize visibility.

• Write high-converting copy that balances aggressive SEO with human readability.

• Design and implement A+ Content (Enhanced Brand Content) to drive significant sales lifts.

• Analyze low Conversion Rates (CR) and implement critical content changes to improve performance.

4. Quality Control & Prioritization

• Maintain a meticulous personal checklist to ensure 100?curacy in product data before publishing.

• Manage a high-volume queue, prioritizing tasks such as fixing suppressed ASINs over new listing creation when necessary to protect the bottom line.



Required Qualifications & Skills

• Amazon Expertise: Expert-level proficiency in Amazon Seller Central, inventory flat files, and variation logic.

• AI & Technical Skill: Proven experience using AI tools for content generation and PDF editing/manipulation.

• Policy Knowledge: Deep understanding of Amazon compliance to maintain a strong Account Health Rating.

• Strategic SEO: Advanced knowledge of Amazon’s search algorithm and keyword placement.

• Meticulous Execution: A highly reliable work ethic with a focus on precision to avoid costly data mistakes.

title: Operations Coordinator / Virtual Assistant (Remote, Part-Time)
description:
Role Overview

This is not a task-list role. We are looking for a self-directed operations coordinator who takes ownership of a short-term rental property and a personal operations portfolio — and runs both with minimal oversight.



You will manage two domains: (1) full-cycle operations for a high-performing Airbnb property in St. Pete, FL, and (2) personal administrative and logistics support for a senior finance executive. Both areas require independent judgment, proactive decision-making, and a system-builder mindset.



The right candidate thinks in systems, not tasks. You will be expected to identify issues before they escalate, improve the processes you inherit, and operate as though you are accountable for outcomes — not just completion of instructions.



Schedule & Availability

This role is outcome-based, not time-based. There are no fixed office hours.



You manage your own schedule around the operational rhythm of the property: guest check-ins and checkouts, cleaner schedules, vendor visits, and maintenance windows. Most active operational days are Sundays (primary turn day) and Mondays. Midweek days are typically lighter.



• Estimated commitment: 20 hours/week (part-time)

• You must be available and responsive during Eastern Time business hours, especially on turn days

• Urgent situations — guest-impacting issues, cleaner no-shows, maintenance emergencies — require same-day response regardless of day or hour

• Communication with the owner is via WhatsApp for approvals and escalations; response expected within 2 hours during ET business hours



Part 1: Property Operations

What You Own

You are the operational owner of the property between guest stays and throughout each turnover cycle. The goal is a property that runs without owner intervention except for true exceptions.



• Own the full turnover cycle end-to-end: confirm cleaner arrival after checkout, verify no visible damage immediately, review cleaning photos same day (or flag as non-urgent if no guest impact)

• Prioritize issues by urgency: same-day guest impact = handle immediately; non-guest-impacting = next-day resolution acceptable

• Maintain a master operations calendar covering guest stays, cleaner schedules, vendor visits, pool service, landscaper dates, maintenance appointments, and delivery windows — with completion notes and issue flags

• Monitor the Airbnb calendar daily for new bookings; schedule cleaning with the appropriate company and note same-day turn status explicitly

• Coordinate two cleaning vendors (primary and backup); manage scheduling, photo review, feedback, and escalation

• Review before/after cleaning photos for quality; follow up the same day if photos are missing or issues are visible

• Monitor the supply request log; place Instacart (Costco) and Amazon orders proactively — do not wait for items to run out

• Monitor all smart home systems weekly: Schlage locks, Ring cameras, Honeywell thermostat, Raymote pool heater

• Handle all routine guest communication via Hospitable; use AI-assisted drafts as a starting point; escalate non-standard situations to owner with a recommended response

• Manage pool heat requests end-to-end: confirm payment, activate via Raymote, maintain temperature within defined limits

• Send a Monday weekly operations update every week by 10:00 AM ET



Revenue Awareness

This role includes a revenue support dimension. You are not in sales — but you are expected to recognize and act on straightforward opportunities within defined rules.



• Identify adjacent open nights next to existing reservations and proactively reach out to guests with a 15% discount offer

• Manage early check-in and late checkout requests as revenue line items, not just scheduling decisions

• Flag recurring patterns in guest behavior or requests that may indicate pricing, listing, or process improvements

• Support execution of simple revenue optimizations as directed by the owner



Maintenance Coordination

• Classify every issue as urgent (same-day guest impact) or non-urgent (next-day or scheduled resolution)

• Urgent issues: use fast-response vendors (Thumbtack) and escalate to owner simultaneously

• Non-urgent issues: gather 2–3 quotes from licensed, highly-rated vendors; present a clear recommendation with reasoning

• Log all maintenance items with status, vendor, quote, and resolution date



Part 2: Personal Operations

You act as a Personal Operations Manager for the principal — not a reactive inbox monitor. This means managing administrative tasks end-to-end, following up without being asked, and keeping every open loop organized and moving toward resolution.



• Own all open loops: track deadlines, follow-ups, and pending items in a shared tracker with owners and due dates

• Follow up with service providers, lawyers, bill disputes, and administrative items without waiting for a reminder

• Handle life logistics: appointments, scheduling, calls, research, and coordination

• Organize Google Drive and all documents per established structure; file receipts and correspondence immediately

• Draft communications for owner review; handle routine vendor and administrative correspondence independently

• Research and shortlist vendors or options with structured summaries ready for a decision

• Prepare briefing notes or agendas as needed



System Building

This is a system-building role, not just an execution role. We expect the person in this position to leave the operation more organized and more transferable than they found it.



• Maintain and continuously improve the operations SOP — update it when processes change, when edge cases arise, or when a better approach is identified

• Document workflows clearly enough that a future coordinator could take over with minimal transition time

• Organize all systems, trackers, and files so that nothing lives only in your head

• Propose process improvements proactively; flag inefficiencies when you see them



What Success Looks Like

We define success clearly. After 30 days, you should be operating fully independently. After 90 days, the standard is:



Area Standard

Property operations Property runs without owner intervention. No missed turnovers, no unresolved guest issues, no gaps in photo review or supply management.

Communication Owner receives a Monday update every week. Escalations are infrequent, well-contextualized, and come with a recommended solution.

Systems Master tracker is accurate and current. SOP reflects how things actually work. Drive is organized. Nothing falls through the cracks.

Revenue support Upsell opportunities are identified and acted on consistently. Add-on payments are collected and logged correctly.

Personal operations Open loops are tracked, followed up on, and closed without reminders. Admin tasks are handled end-to-end.

Independence Fully autonomous within defined rules within 30 days. Escalations are exception-based, not routine.



What We’re Looking For

• Minimum 2 years in a remote operations, property management, or executive assistant role

• Demonstrated ability to manage workflows independently without daily oversight

• Strong written English: clear, concise, professional

• Comfortable with Google Workspace, Airbnb, Hospitable, and smart home platforms

• Experience with short-term rental or property operations is a strong advantage

• Owner mindset: you treat the operation as your own and take accountability for outcomes

• Systems thinker: you document, improve, and organize as a default behavior, not an afterthought

• Discreet and trustworthy: handles sensitive access and information with professionalism



What We Offer

• A role with real responsibility and genuine autonomy — not a checklist

• Clear systems, documented processes, and a principal who values your time and judgment

• Stable long-term engagement with defined growth path

• Full remote, flexible schedule built around outcomes



Compensation

Starting rate: $600 – $700 USD/month (part-time, ~20 hrs/week)



• 90-Day Performance Review: Formal review after 90 days. Strong performers move to $700–$750/month. We reward ownership, consistency, and outcomes — not hours logged.

• 13th Month Bonus: Paid each December as recognition of commitment and performance over the year.

• Annual raise: Considered after Year 1 based on performance and scope growth.

• Payment schedule: Weekly for the first two months; bi-monthly or monthly thereafter by mutual agreement.



How to Apply

Apply through OnlineJobs.ph. In your application, include:



• A brief summary of a system or process you built or improved in a previous role

• Your experience with short-term rentals, property management, or similar operations (if applicable)

• One example of a situation where you identified and resolved an issue before it was escalated to you



Applications without a personal note will not be reviewed. We are looking for operators, not applicants.

title: Freelance Credit Control Team (Associate to Senior Officer) – Philippines (Remote)

Freelance Credit Control Team (Remote, Philippines-based, Flexible Hours)



Credit Control Associate, Credit Controller, Senior Credit Control Officer



About Us

The Credit Collections Company Ltd is a UK-based, SME-focused credit management firm. We support clients with credit control and debt recovery using a warm, professional and relationship-led approach.



The Roles

We’re building a flexible freelance pool in the Philippines to support both PAYG (Pay As You Grow) and monthly-credit-control clients. Some clients need one-off support, and others need more regular help. We are recruiting approximately 2 people for each level.

This role suits someone who already has a full-time job and wants to supplement their income, or someone looking for long-term remote work with the opportunity to grow.



Pay (paid hourly in PHP)

Credit Control Associate: ?115/hour (0–2 years’ relevant experience)



Credit Controller: ?173/hour (2–3 years’ credit control/collections experience)



Senior Credit Control Officer: ?202/hour (3–5 years’ credit control/collections experience)



All payments are made in Philippine pesos (PHP), so you never lose out on exchange rates.



What You’ll Do

Chase overdue balances following the agreed client criteria

Maintain accurate records and provide regular updates

Build supportive client relationships; be firm when needed

Escalate queries/unresolved issues to management

Uphold high standards of professionalism and compliance

Support other reasonable office tasks as needed



Requirements

Experience appropriate to the level you are applying for

English proficiency at C1 (Advanced) or C2 (Mastery/Proficient) level is required

Confident with Microsoft Office (Outlook, Excel, Word)

Reliable internet and home office setup

Friendly, empathetic, organised, and calm under pressure



Working Hours & Flexibility

All work (calls and admin) must be completed between 9 am and 6 pm UK time. Typical demand is 2–4 hours per day, but this can vary depending on client needs. No guaranteed minimum hours.



Progression

For the right committed candidate who looks after clients well, you may be considered for a permanent role in future with additional perks (e.g., paid holidays and sick pay). We don't guarantee this, but we love to promote from within.



How to Apply

title: Property Operations Lead - Remote
description:
Location: Remote 

Schedule: Monday–Friday, 8:00am–6:00pm Eastern Time (USA) 

Compensation: $6/hour + monthly performance bonus 



SteelSafe Self Storage runs remote, online-first storage: rent online, get instant access, move in today. That only works when units are clean, functional, and ready to rent fast. When turns stall, inventory dies. 



We’re hiring a Property Manager with one scoreboard: more rent-ready units, faster turns. 



What you get: 

A clear scoreboard and a role that matters. 

High trust and real autonomy. 

Direct access to leadership and fast decisions. 

A system-driven operation (we run on Asana). 

Performance bonus opportunity tied to ops execution.

Run fast turns: Unit move?outs to Ready?to?Rent (R2R). 

Manage boots on the ground(BOTG) contractors and vendors. 

Create and drive work orders to completion with proof (photos/video). 

Own site readiness: gates, locks, cameras, lighting, cleanliness, curb appeal. 

Handle property projects end?to?end (example: order a sign, coordinate install, confirm with proof).  



What you own (outcomes):

Rent?Ready Unit % stays high and trending up.

The authority and tools you’ll have:

You can approve vendor spend up to $50 per job. Above that requires approval before work begins. 

Asana as the operating system (tasks, checklists, proof).

Think in checklists, photos, and deadlines.

title: Operations Team Lead (Remote)
description:
Operations Team Lead(Remote) for SteelSafe Self Storage



Pay: $7/hour (full-time)

Schedule: 8:00 AM–6:00 PM EST (Mon–Fri), 1 hour lunch

Work Location: Remote (Philippines)





At SteelSafe we get things get finished.

Leads don’t sit.

Follow-ups don’t disappear.

Customer problems don’t get “noted” and forgotten.



If you’re the type of manager who needs constant reminders or gives status updates without results, this won’t be a fit.



If you like building a strong team, coaching people to improve, and running a clean system where nothing falls through the cracks, keep reading.



What you’ll be doing

-You’ll manage a team of remote CSRs in the Philippines and help us build a reliable sales and service engine.

-You will also take calls. Our volume is manageable (usually around 15 calls per CSR per day). We expect you to lead by example, not just by telling people what to do.



What you’ll own (non-negotiables)

-You will make sure no rentals are missed. Every day you will check every lead source and make sure every opportunity has a real outcome.

-You will review website form submissions and confirm whether they rented. If they did not rent, you will call them, log them properly in CallPotential, and document the result and next step.

-You will review CallPotential leads and reservations daily, make sure the status is correct (Lead vs Reservation), verify outcomes, and follow up to close.

-You will review inbound sales calls and make sure non-renters are called back with a real attempt to convert, and you will document what happened.

-Every lead must end the day as one of three things: rented, didn’t rent (with the reason), or a scheduled follow-up with a specific date and time.

-You will run a real call review and coaching system. This is not “we’ll review calls when we have time.”

-The expectation is that you review sales calls daily (or meet a minimum standard if call volume spikes), and you review at least five service calls per day looking for service recovery issues.

-When calls are weak—missed close, wrong information, tone problems, or a bad customer experience—you will send short, clear coaching feedback, coach the CSR directly, and make sure the correction shows up within the next 24–48 hours.

-You will also save coaching examples into a simple training library so the whole team improves over time.

-You will handle escalations and service recovery.

-If someone is upset, did not rent because of the experience, or has a sensitive issue, you will personally follow up, fix the issue, and turn it into a win when possible.

-You will make sure escalations are actually resolved, not just “handled.”

-You will set the standard, train people, enforce quality, and hold the line. That includes hiring and training when needed, assigning tasks and expectations clearly, correcting problems quickly when performance slips, and keeping the team professional and consistent.

-A non-negotiable part of this job is “close-the-loop” control.

-You will review each CSR’s daily update. We do not accept status-only updates.

-Every item must have a result or a next step.

-Anything labeled “pending customer response” must have a follow-up plan and a scheduled date.

-If something is not confirmed complete, it must be assigned a next action with an owner and a due time.

-No dropped balls. No silent failures.



Reporting (daily + weekly)

-Each day you will post a simple daily report so leadership has clarity and the team stays sharp.

-That daily report must include: forms reviewed, leads and reservations reviewed, new rentals closed, open follow-ups, calls listened to, calls flagged, the patterns you are seeing, which CSRs missed closes, what coaching was given, unhappy customers identified and recovered, anything left open, and tomorrow’s focus.

-Each week you will produce a scorecard showing leads vs rentals and conversion percent by source (website, CallPotential, inbound calls), performance by CSR (calls, rentals, conversion percent), top wins, and the top focus areas for next week.





You’re a great fit if…

You take ownership and solve problems instead of passing them around.

You’re a real coach who is clear and helpful, but firm when needed.

You like numbers and performance tracking.

Your spoken and written English is excellent, and you’re confident on the phone.

You can lead with high standards without being toxic.



Small business reality

Random urgent things will come up sometimes. We want someone who stays calm, proposes options, makes decisions, and follows through.

This is not an executive assistant role, but it is a role for someone who solves problems.



How to apply (required)

title: Lead Video Editor AND AI Creator for unique Youtube Channels
description:
Here is the updated version with the availability requirement clearly stated!



***



## **Job Title: Lead AI Video Editor & Retention Specialist**



**About Us & The Role:**

We run a fast-growing network of YouTube channels focused on highly cinematic, emotional AI storytelling and commercial content. We are pushing the boundaries of AI media, but our ultimate metric is viewer satisfaction. We’re looking for a visionary AI Creator and Video Editor who speaks the language of YouTube. You obsess over the retention graph, craft irresistible hooks, and create unique, special content that keeps viewers HOOKED and watching till the absolute end. There is SO much opportunity here, and we’re incredibly excited to bring the right person onto the team!



**What You’ll Be Doing:**

* **Retention-Obsessed Editing:** Assemble and edit emotional AI films with a strict focus on pacing, dynamic cuts, and audio/visual syncing to drive Average View Duration (AVD) through the roof.

* **Crafting the Hook:** Engineer the first 5-10 seconds of every video to be absolutely unskippable.

* **AI Content Generation:** Masterfully prompt and generate ultra-realistic assets using top-tier tools (Higgsfield, Nano Banana, HeyGen, Midjourney, etc.).

* **R&D & Innovation:** Stay on the bleeding edge of AI tech and YouTube trends to keep our content fresh and wildly engaging.



**What We’re Looking For:**

* **Availability:** You **MUST** be available for part-time work immediately, with the ability and desire to eventually transition into a full-time role.

* **YouTube Brain:** You understand audience psychology, AVD, and exactly what makes a viewer commit to a video.

* **Expert Editing Skills:** An impeccable eye for pacing, lighting, and cinematic composition using professional editing software.

* **AI Proficiency:** Proven hands-on experience generating high-quality content using platforms like Higgsfield, HeyGen, and Nano Banana.

* **Proactive Communicator:** You are a self-starter, easy to communicate with, and take absolute ownership of the final product.



**What We Offer:**

* A reasonable and competitive salary based on experience.

* Massive room for creative freedom and career growth as our channels scale and conquer the algorithm.



**How to Apply:**

Please send us your portfolio with direct links to your AI-generated videos or YouTube edits! *(Bonus points if you can share Specific Video LINKS that you have worked on!)* Applications without a portfolio of relevant past work will most likely not be considered.
